2604.16988.title: In-Context Learning Under Regime Change
2604.16988.paper_summary: Non-stationary sequences arise naturally in control, forecasting, and decision-making. The data-generating process shifts at unknown times, and models must detect the change, discard or downweight obsolete evidence, and adapt to new dynamics on the fly. Transformer-based foundation models increasingly rely on in-context learning for time series forecasting, tabular prediction, and continuous control. As these models are deployed in non-stationary environments, understanding their ability to detect and adapt to regime shifts is important. We formalize this as an in-context change-point detection problem and formally establish the existence of transformer models that solve this problem. Our construction demonstrates that model complexity, in layers and parameters, depends on the level of information available about the change-point location, from no knowledge to knowing exact timing. We validate our results with experiments on synthetic linear regression and linear dynamical systems, where trained transformers match the performance of optimal baselines across information levels. We also show that encoding and incorporating changepoint knowledge indeed improves the real-world performance of a pretrained foundation models on infectious disease forecasting and on financial volatility forecasting around Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) announcements without retraining, demonstrating practical applicability to real-world regime changes.
2604.16988.paper_summary_zh: 非平穩序列在控制、預測和決策中自然產生。數據生成過程在未知時間點發生變化，模型必須檢測變化，丟棄或降低過時證據的權重，並即時適應新的動態。基於Transformer的基礎模型越來越依賴於上下文學習來進行時間序列預測、表格預測和連續控制。隨著這些模型在非平穩環境中部署，理解它們檢測和適應制度變化的能力變得重要。我們將此形式化為一個上下文變更點檢測問題，並正式確立解決此問題的Transformer模型的存在。我們的構建展示了模型的複雜性，無論是在層數還是參數上，都取決於關於變更點位置的可用信息水平，從無知識到知道確切的時間。 我們通過對合成線性回歸和線性動態系統的實驗來驗證我們的結果，其中訓練過的Transformer在各種信息水平上與最佳基準的性能相匹配。我們還展示了編碼和納入變更點知識確實改善了預訓練基礎模型在傳染病預測和在聯邦公開市場委員會（FOMC）公告周圍的金融波動預測中的實際表現，而無需重新訓練，顯示出對現實世界制度變化的實用適用性。
2604.16988.author: Carson Dudley et.al.
2604.16988.authors: Carson Dudley, Yutong Bi, Xiaofeng Liu, Samet Oymak
